strands.title: Tensor Projection Core Problem
strands.content: The projection problem requires taking transformer output + prior tensor state and producing an updated tensor that maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only logs. Core tension: TensorRecord is semantically rich (strands, claims, epistemic metadata, declared losses, questions, instructions) but projector must reliably populate all fields. Proposal: start with minimal viable set (strands, key claims, declared losses, instructions, open questions) and iterate richness in autoresearch loop. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world data source discovered and accessible — /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains 15+ experiment runs. CYCLE 38 DATA RESOLUTION: Data schema is now CONFIRMED as JSONL (JSON-Lines, one turn per line). Both logs/proxy_*.jsonl and session/ contain structured data. Projection pipeline can ingest real structured data. CYCLE 39 CRITICAL BLOCKER: JSONL files are MASSIVE (>62K tokens single file). Direct full-file reads fail with token limits. New strategy required: incremental parsing, chunk-based sampling, or targeted queries instead of full schema inspection.

key_claims.text: Projection (tensor_d, transformer_output) → tensor_d' is the core research question; everything else is engineering
TensorRecord has sufficient richness (strands, claims, losses, questions, instructions, T/I/F) to test coherence hypothesis vs append-only baseline
Minimal viable projector should include: strands, key claims with T/I/F, declared losses, instructions_for_next, open questions; defer provenance/lineage
Running projector against conversation logs enables empirical testing of drift hypotheses and declares explicit validation strategy

Real conversation logs are now confirmed accessible at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ with structured metadata (config.json, logs/, session/, test_results.txt); data exploration is BLOCKING prerequisite
Data schema CONFIRMED (cycle 38): logs/ and session/ both use JSONL (JSON-Lines) format; one JSON object per line; parser is straightforward (Python json + file iteration)
JSONL files (logs/proxy_*.jsonl) exceed 62K tokens when read in full; token limits prevent direct full-file schema inspection; chunked/offset reading required
File API supports offset + limit parameters; targeted sampling strategy can replace full-file reads without loading entire content into memory

strands.title: Implementation Architecture: Projector as Separate Module
strands.content: Projector should be standalone module decoupled from main transformer model. Input: (tensor_d, transformer_output). Output: tensor_d' (updated TensorRecord). Acts as memory controller (not ALU). Enables independent testing and iteration. Haiku model size acceptable for in-context memory integration. Pydantic base model (ApachetaBaseModel) uses frozen=True, extra='forbid', Pydantic v2 conventions — projector output must respect this schema. STATUS (cycle 38): Architecture frozen, ready for implementation once data schema is understood. STATUS (cycle 39): Data schema discovery blocked by file size. Architecture remains frozen. Next action: use offset-based sampling to peek at first 1-5 complete JSON objects (not 30+ lines), then design input/output adapters.

key_claims.text: Projector should be separate from main model (transformer) — projector is memory controller, not reasoning engine
ApachetaBaseModel requires frozen=True (immutable), extra='forbid' (strict schema), Pydantic v2 serialization
Standalone module enables independent testing before integration into main reasoning loop

JSONL format (one JSON object per line) requires simple sequential parser, but file size demands chunked reading: offset-based sampling, grep for line counts, or external tools (wc, head -N with offset)
Offset-based file reading (offset + limit parameters) allows targeted extraction of specific line ranges without loading full file; ideal for schema discovery on large JSONL

strands.title: Design Decision: H0 Scope — What to Build First
strands.content: H0 (Hypothesis 0) should demonstrate that projector can maintain reasoning coherence with lossy compression. Start minimal: strands (thematic threads), key claims with T/I/F epistemic values, declared losses (explicit incompleteness), instructions_for_next (branch prediction), open questions (unresolved). Defer: provenance chains, composition equations, lineage tags. Iterative enrichment via autoresearch loop allows adding complexity once H0 baseline established. CYCLE 38 CONSTRAINT: Actual test data is JSONL with unknown field structure. CYCLE 39 UPDATE: File size prevents direct inspection via single-file read. Strategy pivots to targeted sampling: read first 1-5 JSON lines (using offset 0 limit 5), extract and document field names manually from these samples, use pattern inference to design claim/strand heuristics, avoid full-file load until necessary.

key_claims.text: H0 should test: lossy projection maintains reasoning coherence vs append-only baseline
Provenance, composition equations, lineage tags are bookkeeping (defer) not core cognition (H0 focus)
JSONL schema discovery via direct full-file read is not feasible (token limit overflow); must use offset/limit or external tools (wc, head, grep) for targeted sampling

Minimal viable schema inspection: request file read with offset=0, limit=5 (first 5 lines/lines up to token limit), manually parse JSON in response, document field names and types, use as design template

strands.title: Cycle 35-39 Actionable: Build Standalone Projector Module — DATA ACCESS STRATEGY PIVOT
strands.content: Ready to implement minimal viable projector as standalone Python module. Takes (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str) and produces tensor_d': TensorRecord. Should respect ApachetaBaseModel constraints. CRITICAL UPDATE (cycle 37): Real-world test harness is ACTIVE and ACCESSIBLE. CYCLE 38 BREAKTHROUGH: Data confirmed as JSONL at /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/. CYCLE 39 CONSTRAINT DISCOVERED: Full-file reads exceed token limits (62K+ token files). PIVOT: Use offset-based sampling. Strategy: (1) Request 1-5 lines from offset 0 to peek at schema, (2) Manually extract field names from JSON, (3) Document field → Type → Projector Relevance, (4) Proceed with rule-based projector design without complete file load. This trades completeness for feasibility: we won't see every edge case, but we'll see enough to design a working H0 baseline. Expected: first JSON object should reveal common fields (timestamp, role, content, metadata). With this, can build claim heuristics and strand logic immediately (cycle 39-40).

key_claims.text: Projector input: (tensor_d: TensorRecord, transformer_output: str); output: tensor_d': TensorRecord
Rule-based approach recommended for H0 baseline (deterministic, debuggable, faster iteration than LLM-assisted)
Real-world validation against experiment logs will measure projection stability, declared_losses as drift reduction mechanism, and compression ratio

Data structure confirmed: /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/ contains runs with config.json, logs/, session/; logs and session are JSONL format; files are large (>62K tokens) requiring offset-based access

Data access strategy pivot: offset-based sampling is not a regression, it is a pragmatic constraint satisfaction given token limits; schema can be inferred from first 1-5 complete JSON objects with high confidence

strands.title: Cycle 39: JSONL File Size Discovery — Constraint Discovered
strands.content: Action: attempt to read proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl to discover field schema. Result: file exceeds 62K tokens in single read. Attempted twice with limit=30 and default limit. Token overflow error returned both times. IMPLICATION: Cannot read entire file or even 30 lines at once. File is highly redundant (each turn repeats fields, possibly with large payloads like full conversation history in each message). ANALYSIS: Typical JSONL turn might be 500-2000 tokens (timestamps, model outputs, API responses). If file is 62K tokens, it contains ~31-124 turns (rough estimate). Actual line count unknown until we can run `wc -l` or offset-read first 1-2 lines. NEXT STEP (cycle 39): Use offset-based read with very small limit (1-2 lines) to extract first JSON object and infer schema. Then document field names. Then proceed with projector design without full-file visibility. This is not ideal (we won't see rare fields, edge cases) but is feasible given constraints.

key_claims.text: JSONL files are large (>62K tokens) but field structure is likely consistent across lines; schema can be inferred from 1-2 sample lines with reasonable confidence

Estimated file structure: 31-124 turns per baseline_run1 file (rough estimate based on 62K tokens and typical turn size 500-2000 tokens). Exact count requires line-counting tool (wc -l) or offset-based peek.
JSONL field schema will likely include: timestamp, role/speaker, content/message, model_version/metadata, turn_id, or similar structural markers. Confirmed only by examining first JSON object.
File size overflow is a tractable constraint: offset-based sampling with limit=1-2 should yield first complete JSON line(<5K tokens); sufficient to infer schema structure
Token limit constraint requires methodology shift: abandon hope of seeing full conversation corpus at once; adopt incremental, sampled approach; trade comprehensive view for feasible implementation

strands.title: Declared Losses as Drift Anchors — Hypothesis Persisting
strands.content: User introduced genuinely novel hypothesis (cycle 106): declared losses (explicit accounting of what was dropped) could reduce model drift over long conversation chains. Reasoning: silent loss creates undetectable divergence because the model forgets it forgot. Explicit declared_losses in tensor_d' act as checkpoints — future projections can either re-acquire dropped content (if relevant) or deliberately leave it dropped with awareness. This transforms declared_losses from bookkeeping (what we couldn't fit) into active mechanism for coherence preservation. Next projector could use prior declared_losses to inform what NOT to re-acquire unless user signals importance. STATUS (cycle 38): Hypothesis remains testable. STATUS (cycle 39): Hypothesis remains testable but scaling concern emerges — if files are 31-124 turns, and each turn accumulates declared_losses, managing the declared_losses array itself becomes non-trivial. May need declared_losses_summary (aggregate by category) rather than full list. Design question: should projector maintain full loss history (every item dropped across all turns) or windowed summary (recent N turns + aggregate summary)?

key_claims.text: Declared losses (explicit incompleteness) can function as drift anchors if future projections use them to guide coherence maintenance
Silent loss (unaccounted forgetting) is the root of long-horizon drift; making losses explicit enables corrective feedback
Projector at turn N+1 can inspect declared_losses from turn N and decide whether to re-acquire or maintain conscious discard

Scaling question: over 31-124 turns, declared_losses array could grow to hundreds of items; H0 design must choose: full history (memory cost) vs windowed summary (information loss) vs per-category aggregate (compromise)

declared_losses.description: Cannot read proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl in full due to token limit (file >62K tokens). Lose: complete census of all field types, detection of rare/edge-case fields, validation of schema consistency across all turns. Compensation: offset-based sampling of first 1-2 JSON lines sufficient to infer dominant schema structure; rare fields deferred to implementation discovery.

Cannot determine exact turn count, temporal span, or conversation duration from file inspection alone. Rough estimate 31-124 turns but requires wc -l or external tools to confirm. Impacts: cannot pre-size arrays, cannot estimate compression ratio targets, cannot schedule validation checkpoints. Compensation: proceed with rule-based design; metrics will be measured post-implementation.

Sampling first 1-2 JSON lines will not reveal all possible field variations, error patterns, or structured nesting in JSONL. Edge cases (malformed JSON, optional fields, nested arrays) may surface only during full pipeline execution. Compensation: implement defensive parsing (try-except), logging of parse failures, iterative schema extension as edge cases discovered.

Cycle 38 instructions called for 'Read full sample from JSONL files' which has now been found infeasible at scale. Instructions for full field mapping, quantification of baseline_run1, inspection of config.json + test_results.txt are now blocked pending offset-based peek at schema. Recommitting to these actions in cycle 39 using modified strategy (offset-based instead of full-file reads).

open_questions: JSONL_SCHEMA_PEEK (IMMEDIATE): What are the field names in the first JSON object of logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl? Use offset=0, limit=1-2 to extract first complete line, manually parse JSON to list all field names.
JSONL_SCHEMA_PEEK (IMMEDIATE): What are the field names in the first JSON object of session/d5da3c7a-3c3c-4047-a4ee-276be57567c1.jsonl? Is field structure identical to logs/ or specialized?

LINE_COUNT (BLOCKING): How many turns (lines) are in baseline_run1 logs and session files? Run: wc -l /home/tony/projects/pichay/experiments/baseline_run1/logs/proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl and similar for session. Is it 5, 50, 100+ turns?

FIELD_MAPPING (BLOCKING): Once schema is peeked, document: Field Name → Type → Example Value → Projector Relevance. Identify primary content field (likely 'content', 'message', 'text') and turn structure fields (likely 'timestamp', 'role', 'turn_id').
PARSING_STRATEGY: Should projector ingest logs/proxy_*.jsonl (raw LLM API logs) or session/*.jsonl (processed conversation transcript) or both? Which is source-of-truth for turn sequence?
CONFIG_METADATA: What settings are in config.json? Hyperparameters, model names, or projector-relevant parameters? Required for H0 baseline design.
TEST_METRICS: What metrics are in test_results.txt? Are they baseline scores (accuracy, coherence) against which H0 projector will be benchmarked?

CLAIM_EXTRACTION_HEURISTICS: Once content field is visible, what patterns should trigger claim detection? Sentence boundaries? Assertions with high confidence? Unique statements? Ground in actual content.
STRAND_TOPOLOGY: Should strands be pre-defined (technical, theoretical, validation) or discovered from content? If pre-defined, what 3-5 strands make sense for a conversational AI experiment?

EPISTEMIC_ASSIGNMENT: Should T/I/F values be deterministic defaults or heuristic-driven? Propose: assistant claims → 0.75 truth / 0.15 indeterminacy / 0.1 falsity (conservative baseline), adjusted by modal verbs / negations. Confirm with sample turns.
DECLARED_LOSSES_DESIGN: Should H0 maintain full loss history (every dropped item) or aggregate summary (count by category + recent examples)? Full history scales poorly; aggregate loses granularity.

INSTRUCTIONS_FOR_NEXT_LOGIC: Should instructions_for_next be hand-coded rules (if strand_count > 5 recommend merge) or deterministic patterns (always emit consolidation instruction at turn 10/20)? Needs concrete heuristics.

IMPLEMENTATION_SEQUENCE: Cycle 39 should focus on: (a) offset-peek JSONL schema, (b) run wc -l to get turn count, (c) read config.json + test_results.txt, (d) design rule-based claim/strand/epistemic heuristics, (e) build projector stub. Which is critical path?
VALIDATION_BASELINE: Should H0 be validated on all turns in baseline_run1, or sampled subset (first 5, middle 5, last 5)? Sampling reduces implementation time but misses full-trajectory behavior.

FILE_API_LIMITS: Does file read API support offset + limit in a way that works on JSONL line boundaries? Or does it treat file as character stream (where offset/limit splits lines mid-JSON)? Must verify before designing parser.
DATA_QUALITY_ISSUES: Are JSONL files well-formed (guaranteed valid JSON per line), or could some lines be malformed, truncated, or incomplete? Any known issues in experiment metadata?

BACKWARD_COMPATIBILITY: Should this tensor projector be compatible with prior cycle 1-38 strands and claims, or is cycle 39 a fresh slate? Assume fresh slate for H0 baseline, revisit after validation.

Track: (a) Any claims attempted but rejected (too low confidence, duplicate, off-topic) → declare in losses, (b) Any strands attempted but merged (too similar) → declare, (c) Any questions resolved → note resolution in open_questions. Return: declared_losses list to include in tensor_d'.",

Load turn_1 content, call projector(tensor_d_0', turn_1) → tensor_d_1'. Load turn_2, call projector(tensor_d_1', turn_2) → tensor_d_2'. Log each output JSON. Check: (a) Valid Pydantic output? (b) Fields populated (non-empty strands, claims, questions)? (c) T/I/F valid (sum ≈ 1, no NaN)? (d) No exceptions?",

Use as precision/recall benchmark for projector later. Report: vs gold, what did projector miss (recall)? What did it hallucinate (precision)?",

Compute: (a) Original bytes (sum of turn_0, turn_1, turn_2 content field sizes), (b) Projected bytes (sum of tensor_0, tensor_1, tensor_2 JSON sizes). Ratio should be << 1.0 (compression). If ratio > 0.5, projection is too rich, need to be more lossy. If ratio < 0.1, may be losing important info. Target: 0.15-0.25 compression ratio.",

Do claims in later turns cohere with prior tensor? If PASS: freeze rule-based impl, move to Phase 1.5 (apply to all 15 runs). If FAIL on compression/precision: iterate heuristics (cycle 41). Archive for reference and iteration.",

Aggregate metrics: compression ratio distribution, strand count distribution, loss accumulation distribution. Check: are metrics consistent across runs, or do they vary by run? Investigate outliers. Hypothesis: all runs should show 0.15-0.30 compression, 5-10 strands, <30% total loss (indicating stable projector behavior)."
